trim_reasons: BLOWOUT_RISK (spread -9.5) compounds with a VOLATILE tag and a recency-tough defense (l5 96.4 vs l15 109.9, -12.3%) — if DET blows this out early, Cunningham's counting stats get compressed in Q4 garbage time. The structural blowout risk on a volatile scorer at T20 is not fully neutralized by the bounce-back lift.

VOLATILE REB on a BLOWOUT_RISK game (spread -9.5): if DET leads big late, Cunningham's minutes and rebound opportunities shrink, and his post_miss_hr of 59% is the weakest bounce-back rate on the slate. The combination of blowout risk and a slump-persistent stat with below-average bounce-back makes this structurally fragile.

FG_COLD (-18%) triggered a mandatory step-down and VOLATILE tag on PTS — a player already running cold from the field is one difficult-matchup night away from missing even T10 on a tough DET defense. The 72% confidence is at the low end of the viable range, and the FG_COLD signal is the strongest structural warning on the slate for a secondary scorer.

FG_MARGIN_THIN at 4% (well below the 10% safety floor) triggered a mandatory step-down, and trend is down — Thompson is converting at a razor-thin margin above floor on a stat that is already stepped down. A single cold-shooting night where FG% dips further produces a miss even at T10.

FG_MARGIN_THIN at 1% is the thinnest margin on the entire slate — essentially no statistical buffer above the T10 threshold. On a tough LAL defense with a down trend in FG margin, one subpar shooting night flips this miss with near certainty if the margin degrades at all.

VOLATILE tag on REB with a min_floor of 25.1 against the toughest rebounding defense context (HOU opp_defense tough) — the tier was stepped down to T4 precisely because of volatility, and LeBron's wide distribution means a single low-engagement or foul-trouble game easily produces a miss. The 70% confidence is the minimum viable threshold.

BLOWOUT_RISK (spread -8.5) combined with VOLATILE tag and FG_MARGIN_THIN (1%) on a mandatory step-down pick — if CLE builds a large lead early, Mitchell's Q4 minutes and shot attempts compress, and with essentially zero FG margin buffer, any late-game removal produces a miss at T15.

BLOWOUT_RISK (spread -8.5) with FG_MARGIN_THIN (5%) step-down on a secondary scorer — if CLE blows this out, Harden is among the first rotation players rested in Q4, compressing his scoring accumulation. The structural risk of a blowout-game exit for a secondary scorer at even T10 is meaningful at 68% confidence.

FG_COLD (-16%) triggered a mandatory step-down on a VOLATILE scorer in a BLOWOUT_RISK game (spread -8.5) with a min_floor of only 22.0 — three compounding structural risks simultaneously: cold shooter, blowout exit risk, and a minutes floor that allows a single low-usage game to produce a miss. The 68% confidence is at the absolute minimum viable threshold, and the bear case here is structurally stronger than Pick's adjustments capture.

BLOWOUT_RISK (spread -8.5) with a min_floor of 22.0 and trend=down on REB — if CLE runs away with this game, Mobley's minutes and rebounding opportunities are compressed in Q4, and the trend-down signal suggests his rebounding rate is declining even in normal game contexts.

FG_COLD (-17%) mandatory step-down on a VOLATILE scorer at 70% confidence — Ingram is running significantly cold from the field, and even at the stepped-down T10, a continuation of the cold shooting streak against a tough CLE defense puts this at genuine risk. The VOLATILE tag means the distribution is wide enough that sub-10 games are well within the realistic range.

BLOWOUT_RISK (spread -9.5) combined with trend=down on REB and a tough recency defense flag — the tier was stepped down from T8 to T6 due to the blowout penalty, but if DET blows this out, Duren's garbage-time minutes compress rebounding volume even at the conservative T6 threshold. The post_miss_hr of 65% is also below average for REB props.
